name: Castillo de Alarcón — circular by the Río Júcar
name_original: Castillo de Alarcón — circular por el Río Júcar
description: Cycle 61.9 miles (99.6 km) around Castillo de Alarcón and the Río Júcar, a moderate route with 2391 feet (729 metres) elevation gain, offeri
region_intro: Road cycling around Motilla Del Palancar features a landscape characterized by the plains of La Mancha, interspersed with river valleys and the presence of the Alarcón Reservoir. The region offers varied terrain, from rolling hills to flatter sections, suitable for longer road cycling routes. Elevation gains on some routes indicate a mix of gradients, providing diverse challenges for cyclists. Best road cycling routes around Motilla Del Palancar The most popular road cycling route is Alarcón Reservoir Loop from Motilla del Palancar, a 55.8 miles (89.8 km) trail that takes 5 hours 14 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers extensive views of the reservoir and surrounding countryside. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Entry to La Mancha via Iniesta — circular from Motilla del Palancar, a moderate 51.5 miles (82.8 km) path. This route traverses the plains of La Mancha, passing through agricultural areas and small towns. Local road cyclists also love the Castillo de Alarcón — circular by the Río Júcar, a 61.9 miles (99.6 km) trail leading through the Júcar river valley, often completed in about 4 hours 13 minutes. Road cycling around Motilla Del Palancar is defined by open plains, river valleys, and reservoir views. The network offers options for both moderate and difficult ability levels. The routes in Motilla Del Palancar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 2 reviews. More than 30 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Motilla Del Palancar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the typical difficulty level for road cycling routes around Motilla Del Palancar?
A: The road cycling routes in Motilla Del Palancar offer a mix of challenges. You'll find routes ranging from moderate to difficult, with options like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1102688092/entry-to-la-mancha-via-iniesta-circular-from-motilla-del-palancar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Entry to La Mancha via Iniesta — circular from Motilla del Palancar&lt;/a&gt; being moderate, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1102615754/alarcon-reservoir-loop-from-motilla-del-palancar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alarcón Reservoir Loop from Motilla del Palancar&lt;/a&gt; presenting a more difficult challenge.

Q: What are the typical distances and durations for road cycling routes in this region?
A: Road cycling routes around Motilla Del Palancar generally range from approximately 50 to 60 miles (80 to 100 km) in length. Durations can vary, but most routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1103913616/castillo-de-alarcon-circular-by-the-rio-jucar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Alarcón — circular by the Río Júcar&lt;/a&gt;, typically take between 4 to 5 hours to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1102615754/alarcon-reservoir-loop-from-motilla-del-palancar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alarcón Reservoir Loop from Motilla del Palancar&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1102688092/entry-to-la-mancha-via-iniesta-circular-from-motilla-del-palancar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Entry to La Mancha via Iniesta — circular from Motilla del Palancar&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect along the road cycling routes?
A: The routes offer diverse scenery, from the open plains of La Mancha and agricultural areas to river valleys and extensive views of the Alarcón Reservoir. You'll also pass through small towns and potentially see historic structures like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6266927" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de los Tres Ojos&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road biking in Motilla Del Palancar?
A: Given the region's climate, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for road cycling, with milder temperatures. Summers can be quite hot, especially in the open plains, while winters are cooler but often still suitable for riding, though you should check local weather forecasts.

Q: Are there any challenging routes for advanced road cyclists?
A: Absolutely. Experienced riders looking for a challenge will find routes with significant elevation gains. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24537979" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puertecillo de Yemeda – Enguídanos loop from Motilla del Palancar&lt;/a&gt; features over 1100 meters of elevation gain, providing a demanding ride.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Motilla Del Palancar?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the varied terrain, from rolling hills to flatter sections, and the scenic views, particularly those around the Alarcón Reservoir and the Júcar river valley.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or points of interest along the routes?
A: Yes, the region is dotted with interesting sights. You might encounter historical bridges like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6266927" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de los Tres Ojos&lt;/a&gt; or religious sites such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5413601" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgin of the Loma&lt;/a&gt;. The routes also pass through charming settlements like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6062727" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valverdejo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there opportunities for rest stops or cafes along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes pass through small towns and villages, offering opportunities for rest stops, refreshments, and local cafes. These towns are often integrated into the routes, providing convenient breaks during your ride through the plains of La Mancha.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Motilla Del Palancar area?
A: There are several dedicated road cycling routes around Motilla Del Palancar, with komoot currently featuring four distinct tours. These routes offer a good variety of distances and difficulty levels for cyclists to explore.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on these road cycling routes?
A: The terrain is quite varied, characterized by the plains of La Mancha, interspersed with river valleys and the presence of the Alarcón Reservoir. You'll experience a mix of rolling hills and flatter sections, providing diverse challenges and scenic changes throughout your ride.
tips:
Casasimarro is a vibrant, artisanal town with a strong personality, known for both its musical tradition (guitars) and its agricultural production (mushrooms). It combines heritage, culture, and nature in a way that is characteristic of the La Manchuela region of Cuenca.

Casasimarro is famous for its luthier tradition, unique in Castilla-La Mancha, where several generations have built guitars and instruments for nationally and internationally renowned musicians. This identity is reflected in the Monument to the Guitar, an emblem of the town.

In addition to its musical tradition, Casasimarro is known as the mushroom capital, a deeply rooted agricultural activity that is central to its economy and gastronomy.

Beautiful center not only because of its Iglesia Parroquial de Santa Catalina in the old middle of the town but also because close to it, Las Torcas is located. This is a karst basin that has been formed by the wear of water in this type of rock creating a funnel, with walls at the ends and which is known by the people of the area. It is located to the west of the town.

The castle has a square plan, surrounded by a double defensive enclosure, of which it is worth highlighting the keep, with a Renaissance appearance, with battlements topped with arrowheads.
In general, it is a fortification of irregular shape, of great volume, joined by the stone itself to the rest of the wall and fortifications of the city, together constituting the basis of the entire defensive system of Alarcón.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Castillo de Alarcón — circular by the Río Júcar route?
faqs.answer: The terrain on this road cycling route is varied. You'll encounter dramatic canyons and some mountainous sections with significant elevation changes, particularly around the Río Júcar. However, there are also flatter sections that traverse agricultural plains, providing a diverse cycling experience. The surfaces are mostly paved.

faqs.question: What are the main sights and viewpoints along this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route offers spectacular scenery, including the dramatic gorges of the Río Júcar and panoramic views of the historic Alarcón Castle. You'll also pass through charming towns like Casasimarro and Sisante, and enjoy views of lush riverbanks, forests, and fields.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner road cyclists?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate due to its distance of nearly 100 km and over 700 meters of elevation gain. While there are flatter sections, the varied terrain with significant ascents might be challenging for absolute beginners. Cyclists with a good level of fitness and some experience with longer rides will find it more enjoyable.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas for the start of this exact circular route are not detailed, Alarcón itself has parking options. Roads around Alarcón that descend to the Río Júcar are often well-developed and have low traffic, making them potential starting points. It's advisable to check local parking regulations upon arrival.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is generally during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) when temperatures are milder and the scenery is vibrant. Summers can be very hot in this region, and winters can be chilly, though cycling is possible year-round with appropriate gear.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route. It utilizes public roads and paths.

faqs.question: Is the Castillo de Alarcón — circular by the Río Júcar route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route primarily on paved surfaces. While dogs are generally allowed in public spaces, bringing a dog on a 100 km road cycling trip would require specific arrangements, such as a bike trailer. There are no specific restrictions mentioned for dogs on the route itself, but consider the length and nature of the ride.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other official cycling trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route briefly intersects with a few other official trails. You'll find small sections that overlap with the Vuelta a la Manchuela, Camino de Villalgordo del Júcar, Camino del Humilladero, Cañada Real Andalucía, Camino VI, Camino de Valverdejo, and Cañada Real de los Serranos.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes through several towns and villages, including Casasimarro and Sisante, where you can find cafes, restaurants, and potentially accommodation. The town of Alarcón itself, with its castle, also offers dining and lodging options, including the parador within the castle.

faqs.question: Are there any wild swimming spots near the Río Júcar along the route?
faqs.answer: While the Río Júcar is a prominent feature of the route, specific wild swimming spots are not detailed for this particular cycling trail. The river carves through dramatic gorges, and access points for swimming might be limited or require detours. Always exercise caution and check local regulations if considering swimming in natural waters.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a road cycling trip on this route?
faqs.answer: For this moderate-difficulty road cycling route, you should bring a well-maintained racebike, appropriate cycling attire, a helmet, sufficient water and snacks, a repair kit (spare tube, pump, tire levers), navigation tools (like a GPS device or the komoot app), sun protection, and a first-aid kit. Given the length, consider carrying enough supplies for the duration.
